questions.scenario: Customers need a public HTTPS path to reach `devpolaris-orders-api`. Which service family should the team inspect first?

options.text: Traffic entry: DNS, TLS, routing, load balancing, and backend health.
Artifact storage, because container repositories serve customer HTTP requests.
Billing only, because invoices route traffic.
Labels only, because labels terminate TLS.

questions.explanation: Public entry is a traffic job. DNS, TLS, routing, and backend health need to be considered before the exact service mix is chosen.

questions.scenario: The app needs private configuration and a database password should not live in the container image. Which family is most relevant first?

options.text: Identity and secrets: IAM, service accounts, and Secret Manager.

questions.explanation: Private config is an access and secrets problem. Secret Manager stores secrets, and IAM/service accounts decide who can read them.

questions.scenario: Which mappings follow the job-first approach?

options.text: Keep request evidence -> Cloud Logging and Cloud Monitoring.
Pick service names first, then invent app needs later.

questions.explanation: The foundation map starts with app jobs. Service names become useful when they answer a real need.
